Add shuffle_over_same_T_seed parameter for randomizing genome insertion order (#359)

* Add shuffle_same_T_groups_seed option to shuffle rows within same-T groups

After sorting by dstream_T but before exploding, optionally shuffle rows
within same-dstream_T groups using a polars window function. This randomizes
insertion order of genomes with equal generation counts. The option is
exposed through both the Python API and CLI for surface_unpack_reconstruct
and surface_build_tree.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017kYcpseL6jMFYf9kBGDXg9

* Add smoke tests and rename to shuffle_over_same_T_seed

- Add API smoke tests for surface_unpack_reconstruct and
  surface_build_tree with shuffle_over_same_T_seed parameter
- Add CLI smoke tests for --shuffle-over-same-T-seed flag
- Rename parameter from shuffle_same_T_groups_seed to
  shuffle_over_same_T_seed per review feedback
- Fix black formatting issues

https://claude.ai/code/session_017kYcpseL6jMFYf9kBGDXg9

* Set dstream_T sorted hint after sort, avoid lazy conversion in shuffle

Move set_sorted() call to right after the sort and keep shuffle
operating on the eager DataFrame directly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017kYcpseL6jMFYf9kBGDXg9

v0.5.2: AI-generated instance summary in MCP instructions

Adds LLM integration (OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI) to generate a
contextual summary of the BookStack instance at startup. The summary
describes what the knowledge base is about and who uses it, and is
included in MCP instructions so connecting AI assistants have immediate
context. Cached in DB metadata to survive restarts.

New env vars: BSMCP_LLM_PROVIDER, BSMCP_LLM_API_KEY, BSMCP_LLM_MODEL,
BSMCP_SUMMARY_TOKEN_ID/SECRET (falls back to BSMCP_EMBED_TOKEN_*).

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: multi-tenant portal access with PortalAccess__c (#341)

* feat: add multi-tenant portal access with PortalAccess__c

Introduces email-to-entity mapping via PortalAccess__c junction object,
enabling portal users to access multiple NetworkEntities with role-based
permissions (Admin/Member). The API resolves the effective entity from
X-Portal-User header + entityId param, falling back to API key entity
for backward compatibility.

- New PortalAccess__c object (MasterDetail to NetworkEntity__c)
- DeliveryPortalAccessService: getEntitiesForEmail, validateAccess
- DeliveryPublicApiService: resolveEffectiveEntity, /my-entities route
- Permission sets updated for all 3 tiers (Guest, App, Admin)
- Test suite registered DeliveryPortalAccessServiceTest

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move PortalAccess__c fieldPermissions before objectPermissions

Salesforce metadata requires all fieldPermissions elements to be grouped
together before objectPermissions. Fixes deploy parse error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove EmailTxt__c from fieldPermissions (required fields are implicit)

Salesforce does not allow explicit fieldPermissions on required fields.
EmailTxt__c is required on PortalAccess__c so it's automatically accessible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
results: 1.00 — run 7 BEST cost 292.18, 181dB gain, 33.9MHz GBW, 217mW

Extended L3 max to 20µm and W9/W10 max to 1000µm. DE exploited both:
L3=17.9µm (longer channel → lower λ → higher gain), W10=582µm
(wider output → better drive). Power at record low 217mW.

commit.message:
v0.1.18 — real test execution, human review gate, code review phase

- verify-run: runs actual test command via child_process.spawnSync, maps
  real output to TCs; blocks self-reporting integrity gap
- verify-complete: requires notes on all fail/skip results
- approve: isTTY-gated human review checklist before any phase approval
- phase review (optional): independent Code Reviewer persona produces
  04_CODE_REVIEW.md with PASS / CONDITIONAL_PASS / FAIL verdict
- reviewer persona: ROLE + CONSTRAINTS + REASONING for skeptical review
- extractTestIndex: includes given/when/then so Developer LLM implements
  to exact TC contracts, not just titles
- phase4 buildBriefing: debug mode auto-injects failing TCs when
  04_TEST_RESULTS.json has fail entries; renamed section to Test Specs
- run-phase: auto-injects failingTests into phase 4 briefing
- 196 tests passing (was 103)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Design: revise test plan — focus on existing test updates, trim new tests

Restructured per reviewer feedback:
- Section 1: 20 existing tests to update (field access + behavioral + migration)
- Section 2: 5 new tests for genuinely new behavior (filtering, DiffFilingMetadata, output size)
- Section 3: Rationale for ~40 removed tests (duplicated existing coverage)
- Removed BDD acceptance criteria section (covered by concrete tests)

commit.message:
Design: document where ChangeType 'unchanged' still appears in slimmed output

Address PR review question about whether 'unchanged' is still needed
in ChangeType. Added analysis table showing it's eliminated from
paragraph, table, row, and cell levels but retained at section level
and in summary counts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: SageOx <ox@sageox.ai>
SageOx-Session: https://sageox.ai/repo/repo_019cd628-72d1-7484-bc68-f53406632782/sessions/2026-03-10T17-54-galexy-Ox5lZh/view

Replace LICENSE with standard MPL-2.0 text

The previous LICENSE file contained a custom notice that automated license
detection tools could not parse. This replaces it with the standard MPL-2.0
text so that Julia General Registry and other tools can detect the license.

A note about PMPL-1.0-or-later (the author's philosophical extension of
MPL-2.0, currently in development) is appended at the end.

Add Zoho Mail unread badge to email card on dashboard

Polls /api/zoho-unread every 60s to fetch inbox unread count from
Zoho Mail API. Shows red notification badge on the Emails card.
Requires ZOHO_CLIENT_ID, ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET, ZOHO_REFRESH_TOKEN,
and ZOHO_ACCOUNT_ID env vars to be set in Vercel.

commit.message:
docs(research): 技術棧全面研究 — 10 面向並行收集 + master-index + entities

01 iPhone 備份協定（AFC、libimobiledevice、MTP）
02 mDNS/Bonjour 深度研究（bonjour-service、DNS-SD 原理）
03 LAN 檔案傳輸技術（HTTP chunked、斷點續傳、xxHash64）
04 類似工具分析（iMazing、pymobiledevice3、開源方案）
05 iOS 安全沙箱限制（PTP、AFC、USB Restricted Mode）
06 Electron IPC 架構（MessagePort、Worker Thread、大檔案傳輸）
07 macOS 系統整合（Sequoia 權限、SMAppService、launchd）
08 electron-builder 打包（無簽名 DMG、auto-update 限制）
09 背景服務與排程（node-usb hotplug、launchd、powerSaveBlocker）
10 測試策略（vitest mock、Playwright E2E、memfs）
+ master-index（跨面向洞察、技術選型建議）
+ entities（協定關係圖、工具聚合索引）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): fix Google Sign-In — envDir, useGoogleLogin hook, access token flow

Root cause: Vite wasn't loading VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID because .env
lives at monorepo root but Vite defaults to loading from apps/dashboard/.

Changes:
- vite.config.ts: add envDir pointing to monorepo root
- LoginPage.tsx: switch from GoogleLogin component (cross-origin iframe)
  to useGoogleLogin hook (custom button, implicit OAuth flow)
- auth.ts: support both credential (ID token) and accessToken flows
  in POST /auth/google — access token validated via Google userinfo API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement relaxed-prompt experiment pipeline (5 scripts + JSON)

- relaxed_prompts.json: all 29 questions, relaxed v1/v2 prompts + subpart defs
- 00_label_relaxed_v2.py: run 9B on hard questions with relaxed v2 prompt (29.5k calls)
- 01_make_train_test.py: 80/20 stratified splits; skips missing v2 labels gracefully
- 02_embedding_threshold.py: cosine sim threshold tuning + AND combination vs v2 ground truth
- 03_train_slm_subparts.py: ModernBERT-base + LoRA binary classifier per question
- 04_compare.py: embedding vs SLM comparison table + summary by construct type

commit.message:
Close 3 non-vacuousness sorry's (6→3): exp decay, gradient bound, Ampere

- (8) hExpDecay: proved via equilibriumMaxwellian_exp_lower_bound helper
  (normSq v ≤ 3(1+‖v‖)², C = 3/(2T) + max(0,-log prefix))
- (9) hGradBound: proved via fderiv_equilibriumMaxwellian (simp; ring)
  then |vᵢ|/T ≤ (1+‖v‖)/T using norm_le_pi_norm
- (11) hAmpere: curl of B=0 simplifies to 0, fin_cases i <;> simp

3 sorry's remain: (7) Schwartz, (10) Vlasov nullspace, (12) Gauss integral

commit.message:
feat: batch 3 steps 1-9 — webhooks, integrations, compliance, billing, domains, security, marketing site

Step 1 — Webhook integration:
- WebhookDestination + WebhookDeliveryLog models
- Webhooks API with CRUD, delivery with exponential backoff retries (5 max),
  field mapping, delivery logs, test send, and replay functionality

Step 2 — CRM integrations (HubSpot + Salesforce):
- Integration + IntegrationDeliveryLog models
- Provider modules for HubSpot (Contact create via CRM v3) and
  Salesforce (Lead create via REST v59.0) with OAuth2 token management
- Test connection endpoints per provider

Step 3 — Lead distribution (Leadspedia + CAKE):
- Leadspedia provider: configurable endpoint with auth params
- CAKE provider: conversion posting with tracking params
- Shared delivery queue infrastructure with logging

Step 4 — Compliance (TCPA, TrustedForm, Jornaya):
- Consent recording merged into submission metadata
- Compliance data query and CSV export endpoints
- Support for TrustedForm cert URLs and Jornaya tokens

Step 5 — Stripe billing:
- Org model extended with plan, stripeCustomerId, stripeSubId
- Billing API with checkout session, subscription management,
  webhook handler, usage summary, and cancel stubs

Step 6 — Custom subdomain provisioning:
- Org model extended with customDomain, domainVerified
- Domains API with request, DNS instructions, verification,
  removal, and domain-to-org resolution
- Enterprise plan gating

Step 7 — API error handling:
- Global HttpExceptionFilter with consistent error envelope
  (code, message, details, requestId)
- RequestId middleware (X-Request-Id header)
- Request logger middleware (structured JSON logging)

Step 8 — Runtime security + bot protection:
- CSP middleware with permissive frame-ancestors for embeds
- BotProtectionService with Turnstile/reCAPTCHA validation
- In-memory rate limiting (5 submissions/IP/form/minute)

Step 9 — Marketing website:
- Home page with hero, features grid, verticals, CTA
- Features page (9 features with descriptions)
- Pricing page (Free/Pro/Enterprise tiers with comparison table)
- Contact page, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service
- SEO metadata and CSS reset in root layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
